metadata.title:
N. r. AGREES TO
Leave BROWNE


Street OPEN

pages.raw_text:
The grade separation ordinance
of the Northern Pacific railway
as submitted to the council today
provides that Browne st. shall be
left open. This is contrary to the
oft-repeated declaration of E. j.
Cannon, division counsel, who said
that unless his company was allow
ed to close Browne st. the tracks
would remain as they now are un-
til the crack of doom.


In detail the plans provide for a
depression making the floor of the
subway correspondent to city plans
with a grade of five feet in 100 to
the north and south A roadway
of 40 feet is provided, with one
sidewalk and head room of II feet.


1t is not expected that the or
dinance will be given much con-
sidesation tonight but that it will
be referred for discussion to the
committee of the whole. From ut
terances of Mayor Moore and a
number of councilmen it is believed
that in opening Browne st. the
greatest obstacle to the passage of
the grade separation plans has
been eliminated.
